column1: A unified sustainability disclosure framework integrating BRSR · CDP · EcoVadis · GRI  |  Version 1.0  |  © Impactree
Standard Coverage Legend:  🟢 All 4 standards  |  🟡 3 standards  |  🟠 2 standards  |  🔵 1 standard  |  ⚪ No source mapping (reserved indicator)

column4: Impactree Question
column8: Guidance Note

column4: Provide your company's full legal name, corporate identity number (CIN), year of incorporation, registered address, corporate address, email, telephone, and website.
column8: Provide the company's full legal name, CIN/registration number, year of incorporation, registered office address, corporate address, official email, telephone, and company website.
column14: BRSR-1 | BRSR-2 | BRSR-3 | BRSR-4 | BRSR-5 | BRSR-10 | BRSR-11 | BRSR-12 | BRSR-1 | BRSR-2 | BRSR-4 | BRSR-5 | BRSR-6 | BRSR-1 | BRSR-2 | BRSR-3 | BRSR-4 | BRSR-5 | BRSR-6

column4: Describe your company's main business activities and products/services sold, including their NIC codes and percentage contribution to total turnover.
column8: List all main business activities and products/services that together account for at least 90% of total turnover. Include NIC codes and individual turnover contribution percentages.

column4: Report the number and locations of your plants, offices, and operations (national and international), and the markets served.
column6: Number of locations; Number of countries/states
column7: As at end of reporting period
column8: Report the count of plants, offices, and operations by location type (national/international). Include markets served and the contribution of exports as a percentage of total turnover.

column4: State the financial year for which you are reporting, your reporting boundary (standalone or consolidated), and the stock exchanges on which your shares are listed.
column8: State the financial year being reported, whether reporting is on a standalone or consolidated basis, the rationale for the chosen boundary, and the stock exchanges where shares are listed.
column17: GRI-2-2a | GRI-2-2b | GRI-2-2c | GRI-2-2i | GRI-2-3a | GRI-2-3b | GRI-2-3c | GRI-2-3d

column4: List all holding, subsidiary, associate companies, and joint ventures, including ownership percentage and participation in sustainability initiatives.
column7: As at end of reporting period
column8: List all holding, subsidiary, associate companies, and joint ventures. For each, indicate the relationship type, percentage of shares held, and whether they participate in the company's sustainability initiatives.

column4: Identify your key stakeholder groups, describe how you engage with them (channels, frequency, topics discussed), and explain how their input influences your business decisions.
column8: Identify all key stakeholder groups (including vulnerable/marginalized groups). For each, describe communication channels used, frequency of engagement, and key topics or concerns raised and how they were addressed.

column4: Has your organization mapped its value chain, including upstream supply chain commodities and downstream product flows? If so, describe the scope and methodology.
column8: State whether a formal value chain mapping exercise has been conducted. If yes, describe the scope (upstream/downstream), commodities mapped, and methodology used.

column4: Does your organization have sustainability policies in place? If yes, describe the policies, whether they are board-approved, and provide web links where available.
column8: For each sustainability principle or ESG pillar, confirm whether a policy exists, whether it has board approval, and provide the web link. State whether policies extend to value chain partners.
column17: GRI-2-18a | GRI-2-18b | GRI-2-18c | GRI-2-22a | GRI-2-23a | GRI-2-23i | GRI-2-23b | GRI-2-23c | GRI-2-23d | GRI-2-23e | GRI-2-23f | GRI-2-24a | GRI-2-24i

column4: What specific sustainability commitments, goals, and targets has your organization set, with defined timelines? Report on performance against these targets and reasons for any shortfalls.
column8: List specific sustainability goals and targets with defined timelines. For each, report current performance against the target and reasons for any shortfall.
column17: GRI-2-19a | GRI-2-19i | GRI-2-19v | GRI-2-19b | GRI-2-20a | GRI-2-20i | GRI-2-20b | GRI-2-21a | GRI-2-21b | GRI-2-21c

column4: List all external certifications, standards, and labels adopted by your organization (e.g., ISO 14001, ISO 50001, SA 8000, OHSAS) and the principles they are mapped to.
column8: List all active external certifications (e.g., ISO 14001, ISO 50001, SA 8000, OHSAS). For each, specify the issuing body, scope of certification, and the sustainability principle it relates to.

column4: Does your organization formally and publicly endorse any external sustainability initiatives or sets of principles (e.g., UN Global Compact)? If yes, provide details.
column8: List all formal public endorsements of external sustainability initiatives or principles (e.g., UN Global Compact, Science Based Targets, RE100). For each, provide the endorsement date and a reference link or documentation.

column4: Has your organization undergone third-party assurance or an independent external audit of its sustainability data, policies, or practices? If yes, provide the scope, findings, and auditor details.
column8: State whether an independent external assurance or audit of sustainability data has been conducted. If yes, provide the name of the assurance provider, scope of assurance, assurance standard applied (e.g., ISAE 3000), and level (limited/reasonable).

column4: Describe the process used to identify material ESG topics. List material issues identified, indicate whether each is a risk or opportunity, and explain the financial implications.
column8: Describe the process for materiality assessment. List material ESG issues identified, classify each as risk or opportunity, provide rationale, and indicate positive or negative financial implications.
column17: GRI-3-1a | GRI-3-1i | GRI-3-1b | GRI-3-2a | GRI-3-2b | GRI-3-3a | GRI-3-3b | GRI-3-3c | GRI-3-3d | GRI-3-3i | GRI-3-3e | GRI-3-3f

column4: Which sustainability reporting frameworks or standards does your organization use to report ESG metrics (e.g., GRI, BRSR, CDP, TCFD, SASB)? Describe your consolidation approach and reporting boundary.
column8: Identify all sustainability reporting frameworks or standards used (e.g., GRI, BRSR, CDP, TCFD, SASB, ISSB). Describe your consolidation approach (equity share, financial control, operational control) and reporting boundary.
column16: EV-GEN600 | EV-ENV5001 | EV-ENV6001 | EV-ENV610 | EV-LAB4501 | EV-LAB601

column4: Does your organization communicate progress towards the UN Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs)? If yes, which SDGs are most relevant to your operations and what progress has been made?
column8: Identify which UN Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) are most relevant to your operations and value chain. For each relevant SDG, describe specific actions taken and measurable progress achieved in the reporting year.

column4: Describe the board-level structure for oversight of ESG and sustainability issues, including which committees or directors are responsible and the frequency of review.
column8: Describe the board committee or director(s) responsible for ESG oversight. Include frequency of review, topics discussed, and how ESG performance is reported to the board. State whether board members have relevant competency.
column17: GRI-2-9a | GRI-2-9b | GRI-2-9c | GRI-2-9i | GRI-2-10a | GRI-2-10b | GRI-2-10i | GRI-2-11a | GRI-2-11b | GRI-2-12a | GRI-2-12b | GRI-2-12i | GRI-2-12c | GRI-2-14a | GRI-2-14b | GRI-2-17a

column4: Identify executive-level positions responsible for ESG management and decision-making. How is sustainability integrated into senior management responsibilities?
column8: Identify executive-level roles accountable for ESG management. Describe how sustainability objectives are integrated into senior management KPIs, incentive structures, and decision-making processes.

column4: Does your organization have an anti-corruption and anti-bribery policy? Report the percentage of governance body members, employees, and business partners trained on anti-corruption. Report any incidents, disciplinary actions, or confirmed corruption cases.
column8: Confirm whether an anti-corruption/anti-bribery policy exists. Report the percentage of governance body members, employees, and business partners trained. Report the number of confirmed corruption incidents, disciplinary actions taken, and legal proceedings initiated.

column4: Describe mechanisms in place to identify and manage conflicts of interest for board members and Key Management Personnel (KMPs). Report the number of conflict-of-interest complaints received.
column8: Describe mechanisms to identify and manage conflicts of interest for board members and KMPs. Report the number of conflict-of-interest complaints received and resolved during the reporting period.

column4: Report any legal actions or adverse orders related to anti-competitive behavior, monopolistic practices, or market abuse. Describe corrective actions taken.
column8: Report any legal actions, investigations, or adverse regulatory orders related to anti-competitive conduct or abuse of market dominance. Describe corrective actions taken or underway.

column4: Describe your organization's approach to tax governance, tax strategy, country-by-country reporting, and mechanisms for raising concerns about tax-related matters.
column8: Describe the organization's tax strategy, governance framework, and risk management approach. Include country-by-country reporting obligations, mechanisms for raising tax-related concerns, and the assurance process for tax disclosures.

column4: List trade and industry associations your organization is affiliated with. Describe any public policy positions advocated, lobbying activities, and the method and frequency of board review.
column8: List the top trade and industry associations the organization is affiliated with. For each public policy position advocated, describe the method of advocacy, frequency of board review, and whether information is publicly available.

column4: Describe the grievance redressal and whistleblower mechanisms available to all stakeholder groups (employees, workers, communities, investors, customers, value chain partners). Report grievances filed and pending resolution.
column8: Describe the grievance redressal and whistleblower mechanisms available to each stakeholder group. Report the number of grievances filed and pending resolution at year-end, by stakeholder category.
column17: GRI-2-16a | GRI-2-16b | GRI-2-25a | GRI-2-25b | GRI-2-25c | GRI-2-25d | GRI-2-25e | GRI-2-26a | GRI-2-26i

column4: Report all monetary and non-monetary fines, penalties, settlements, or legal actions by regulatory authorities, law enforcement agencies, or judicial institutions during the reporting year.
column8: Report all monetary fines, penalties, settlements, and compounding fees, and non-monetary actions (imprisonment, punishment) imposed by regulatory/judicial authorities. For each, state whether an appeal has been preferred.

column4: Describe the process used to identify, assess, and manage ESG-related risks and opportunities, including the time horizons used (short, medium, long-term).
column8: Describe the process and tools used to identify, assess, and prioritize ESG-related risks and opportunities. Specify the short, medium, and long-term time horizons used, and how outputs inform strategic and business planning.

column4: Identify climate-related financial risks and opportunities (physical, transition, and systemic) with potential to materially affect your organization. Report the financial implications and actions taken to manage them.
column8: For each material climate-related risk or opportunity, describe its type (physical/transition/systemic), likely financial impact, the time horizon, and management approach. Include current and anticipated financial implications.

column4: Identify risks and opportunities related to biodiversity loss and ecosystem degradation that could materially affect your organization. Describe your approach to managing these.
column8: Identify risks and opportunities arising from biodiversity loss, ecosystem degradation, or nature-related dependencies that could materially affect operations or value chain. Describe the management response.

column4: Identify water-related risks (scarcity, quality, regulatory, reputational) affecting your operations or value chain. Report the financial implications and risk management approach.
column8: Identify specific water-related risks (scarcity, quality degradation, flooding, regulatory) affecting operations or supply chain. For each risk, describe the financial implications and risk management approach.

column4: Has your organization used scenario analysis to assess climate or environmental risks? If yes, describe the scenarios used, assumptions, time horizons, and key findings.
column8: Describe the climate or environmental scenarios used, including warming pathway (e.g., 1.5°C, 2°C, 4°C) or other scenario frameworks. Explain the time horizon, key assumptions, and strategic implications identified.

column4: Does your organization have a business continuity and disaster management plan? Describe its scope, coverage, and key elements.
column8: Confirm whether a business continuity and disaster management plan exists. Describe its scope, the hazards or disruptions it covers, testing frequency, and key mitigation measures.

column4: Report your organization's gross direct (Scope 1) GHG emissions in metric tons CO2 equivalent. Include a breakdown by gas type (CO2, CH4, N2O, HFCs, PFCs, SF6, NF3), the consolidation approach, emission factors, and methodology used.
column8: Report gross direct Scope 1 GHG emissions in tCO2e. Break down by gas type (CO2, CH4, N2O, HFCs, PFCs, SF6, NF3). State the consolidation approach, GWP rates used, emission factors, and methodology standard applied.

column4: Report your organization's gross indirect (Scope 2) GHG emissions in metric tons CO2 equivalent, using both market-based and location-based methods where applicable. Include emission factors and methodology.
column8: Report gross indirect Scope 2 GHG emissions using both market-based and location-based methods. Specify the emission factors, contractual instruments used (for market-based), and the methodology standard.

column4: Report your organization's Scope 3 (value chain) GHG emissions in metric tons CO2 equivalent across all relevant categories. Explain any exclusions and the methodology applied.
column8: Report Scope 3 GHG emissions by category (GHG Protocol categories 1–15). For each category, report the emissions figure, data quality, calculation methodology, and reason for any exclusions.

column4: Report your organization's GHG emission intensity per unit of revenue, production output, or other relevant activity metric. Specify the metric used and the calculation methodology.
column8: Report GHG emission intensity using at least one activity-based metric (e.g., tCO2e per INR crore turnover, per unit produced, per employee). Specify which scopes are included and the denominator used.

column4: What are your organization's GHG emission reduction targets (absolute and/or intensity-based)? Report the base year, target year, scope, and progress made towards achieving these targets.
column8: State the base year, base year emissions, target year, and reduction target (absolute and/or intensity-based). Report progress to date, reasons for any deviations, and whether targets are science-based (SBTi aligned).

column4: Describe your organization's climate transition strategy, including planned capital allocation, technology adoption, and business model changes to align with low-carbon pathways.
column8: Describe the organization's transition strategy including planned business model changes, capital expenditure in low-carbon technologies, and timelines for decarbonization milestones.

column4: Does your organization use carbon credits or offsets to meet GHG targets? If yes, describe the volume, type, verification standard, and project details.
column8: Report the volume of carbon credits or offsets used, broken down by type (avoidance vs. removal), project type, vintage year, and verification standard (e.g., Gold Standard, VCS). State the total spend.

column4: Describe specific projects or initiatives implemented to reduce GHG emissions. Report the estimated or achieved emission reductions (in metric tons CO2e) and associated costs or savings.
column8: List specific GHG reduction projects or initiatives. For each, describe the type of initiative, emission reductions achieved or estimated (tCO2e), investment made, and cost savings realized.

column4: Report biogenic CO2 emissions separately. Describe the sources and the methodology used for calculation.
column8: Report biogenic CO2 emissions separately from fossil-fuel GHG emissions. Describe the sources (e.g., biomass combustion, land-use change) and the methodology used for calculation.

column4: Report total energy consumption within your organization in joules or multiples, including a breakdown by electricity, fuel (renewable and non-renewable), heating, cooling, and steam.
column8: Report total energy consumed within the organization broken down by source: electricity, fuel (by type), heating, cooling, and steam. Distinguish purchased vs. self-generated energy. Indicate if independently assured.

column4: Report total energy consumption from renewable sources, broken down by type (solar, wind, hydro, biomass, geothermal, other).
column8: Report energy consumed from renewable sources broken down by type: solar, wind, hydro, biomass, geothermal, and renewable electricity purchased (with or without certificates). Indicate the basis of classification.

column4: Report total energy consumption from non-renewable sources, broken down by fuel type (coal, natural gas, oil, diesel, other).
column8: Report energy consumed from non-renewable sources broken down by fuel type: coal, natural gas, diesel, furnace oil, petrol, and other. Include fuels used for owned and leased vehicles if material.

column4: Report electricity, heating, cooling, and steam sold to external parties during the reporting period.
column8: Report total electricity, heating, cooling, and steam sold to external parties. This is deducted from total energy consumption calculations. Specify the type and purchaser where relevant.

column4: Report your organization's energy intensity per unit of revenue, production, or other relevant metric. Specify the metric used.
column6: GJ per INR crore / GJ per unit produced
column8: Report energy intensity using at least one metric (energy per unit of revenue, per unit of output, or other relevant denominator). Clearly specify the denominator and which energy types are included.

column4: What are your organization's energy reduction targets? Report progress against these targets, including PAT Scheme compliance where applicable.
column8: State energy reduction targets (absolute and/or intensity-based). Report progress to date. If applicable, disclose PAT Scheme designation, assigned targets, and whether targets have been achieved.

column4: Describe initiatives, technologies, and programs implemented to improve energy efficiency or reduce energy demand in operations and/or products.
column6: GJ or MWh saved; INR / USD invested
column8: List specific energy efficiency projects or programs implemented. For each, describe the initiative, technology used, energy saved (GJ or MWh), and financial savings or investment associated.

column4: Report the total volume of water withdrawn from all sources (surface water, groundwater, third-party water, seawater/desalinated water, and other) in kilolitres or megalitres.
column8: Report water withdrawn from each source: surface water, groundwater, third-party water (municipal/utility), seawater/desalinated water, and other. Report total withdrawal. Indicate if independently assured.

column4: Report the total volume of water consumed by your organization during the reporting period.
column8: Report total water consumed (withdrawn minus discharged). Provide a breakdown by facility or region if material. Indicate if any water is consumed outside organizational boundaries.

column4: Report the volume and percentage of water recycled or reused within your organization's operations.
column8: Report total volume of water recycled or reused within the organization. Express as both an absolute volume and as a percentage of total water withdrawal. Describe the recycling systems in place.

column4: Report water discharged by destination (surface water, groundwater, seawater, third-parties) and the level of treatment applied (no treatment, with treatment — specify level).
column8: Report water discharged by destination (surface water, groundwater, sea, third-party) and by level of treatment applied (none, primary, secondary, tertiary). Report total discharge volume. Indicate if independently assured.

column4: Report your organization's water intensity per unit of revenue, production, or other relevant activity metric. Specify the metric used.
column6: KL per INR crore / KL per unit produced
column8: Report water intensity using at least one activity metric. Specify the denominator (revenue, production, area) and the scope of water included (withdrawal or consumption).

column3: Water Withdrawal & Discharge in Stressed Areas
column4: For operations in water-stressed areas, report water withdrawal, consumption, and discharge data separately by facility or plant location.
column8: For each facility located in a water-stressed area (per WRI Aqueduct or equivalent), report water withdrawal by source, total consumption, and discharge by destination and treatment level. Name the area and nature of operations.

column4: Describe minimum standards set for the quality of effluent discharged, including how standards are applied in locations with no local discharge requirements.
column8: Describe minimum quality standards set for effluent discharge. Include parameters monitored (BOD, COD, TSS, heavy metals, etc.), applicable regulatory limits, and how standards are applied in locations without local requirements.

column4: Has your organization implemented a Zero Liquid Discharge (ZLD) mechanism? If yes, describe its scope and coverage.
column8: State whether Zero Liquid Discharge (ZLD) has been implemented. If yes, describe the plants or facilities covered, the technology used, volume of water recycled through ZLD, and compliance with relevant regulatory requirements.

column4: What are your organization's water-related targets (reduction, recycling, efficiency)? Report progress against these targets and timelines.
column8: State specific water-related targets (reduction in withdrawal, increase in recycling, efficiency improvement). For each target, report the base year, target year, baseline value, and current performance.

column3: Interactions with Water as a Shared Resource
column4: Describe how your organization interacts with water as a shared resource, including water-related impacts, how they are identified, and how they are addressed.
column8: Describe how the organization interacts with and depends on shared water resources. Explain the approach to identifying water-related impacts, stakeholder engagement on water, and mitigation or adaptation measures applied.

column4: Report total waste generated by your organization, broken down by type: plastic waste, e-waste, bio-medical waste, construction and demolition waste, battery waste, radioactive waste, hazardous waste, and non-hazardous waste (in metric tonnes).
column8: Report total waste generated broken down by type: plastic waste, e-waste, bio-medical waste, construction and demolition waste, battery waste, radioactive waste, hazardous waste (specify types), and non-hazardous waste. Indicate if independently assured.

column4: Report total waste diverted from disposal through recycling, reuse, composting, and other recovery operations, by waste category (in metric tonnes).
column8: For each waste category, report the volume diverted from disposal through recycling, reuse, composting, anaerobic digestion, or other recovery operations. Report as both absolute volume and percentage of total waste generated.

column4: Report total waste disposed by disposal method: incineration, landfilling, and other disposal operations, by waste category (in metric tonnes).
column8: For each waste category, report the volume disposed of by disposal method: incineration (with and without energy recovery), landfilling, deep-well injection, and other disposal methods.

column4: Describe your strategy and practices for managing hazardous and toxic waste, including reduction targets, handling procedures, and regulatory compliance.
column8: Describe the strategy and processes for managing hazardous and toxic waste. Include the types of hazardous waste generated, reduction targets, handling and storage procedures, approved disposal contractors, and regulatory compliance status.

column4: Report the total weight or volume of materials used in production or service delivery, broken down by renewable and non-renewable materials.
column8: Report total materials used in production or service delivery, broken down by material type and classified as renewable or non-renewable. Where possible, specify materials by weight or volume.

column4: What percentage of your input materials are recycled or reused? Report by material type for the current and previous financial year.
column6: % of total input materials (by value or weight)
column8: Report the percentage of input materials sourced as recycled or reclaimed content, by material type. Describe how recycled content is verified and measured.

column4: Describe the processes in place to reclaim and manage products at end of life, including plastics/packaging, e-waste, and hazardous waste.
column8: Describe end-of-life reclaim and management processes for: (a) plastics and packaging, (b) e-waste, (c) hazardous waste, and (d) other categories. Report volumes reclaimed, reused, recycled, and safely disposed.

column4: Is Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) applicable to your organization? If yes, is your waste collection plan aligned with the EPR plan submitted to the relevant Pollution Control Board?
column8: State whether EPR obligations apply to your organization. If yes, describe the product categories covered, the waste collection targets set, actual collection achieved, and whether the EPR plan filed with the Pollution Control Board is being met.

column4: Report reclaimed products and their packaging materials as a percentage of total products sold per product category.
column8: For each major product category, report reclaimed products and their packaging materials as a percentage of total products sold. Describe the take-back or collection mechanisms in place.

column4: Report total NOx emissions by source, in appropriate units, along with the methodology used.
column8: Report total NOx emissions by source (stationary combustion, process, fugitive). Specify the unit of measurement and the measurement or calculation methodology applied.

column4: Report total SOx emissions by source, in appropriate units, along with the methodology used.
column8: Report total SOx emissions by source. Specify the unit and the measurement or estimation methodology applied. Note any SO2 abatement systems in operation.

column4: Report total particulate matter (PM) emissions by source, in appropriate units, along with the methodology used.
column8: Report total PM2.5 and PM10 emissions separately where possible. Specify sources, measurement methodology, and monitoring frequency.

column4: Report total volatile organic compound (VOC) emissions by source, in appropriate units, along with the methodology used.
column8: Report total VOC emissions by source process. Specify the unit of measurement, key VOC compounds emitted where known, and the methodology used.

column4: Report total persistent organic pollutant (POP) emissions by source, in appropriate units, along with the methodology used.
column8: Report total POP emissions or releases (air, water, land) by type (e.g., dioxins, furans, PCBs). Specify the unit of measurement and the identification and measurement methodology.

column4: Report total hazardous air pollutant (HAP) emissions by source, in appropriate units, along with the methodology used.
column8: Report total HAP emissions by compound or compound group. Specify the unit of measurement, regulatory thresholds applicable, and the monitoring or estimation methodology used.

column4: Is your organization compliant with applicable air quality regulations? Report any non-compliances, regulatory actions, monitoring mechanisms, and corrective actions taken.
column6: Number of non-compliances; INR / USD penalties
column8: State whether the organization is compliant with applicable air quality regulations (e.g., Air (Prevention and Control of Pollution) Act or equivalent). Report any non-compliances, monitoring mechanisms deployed, and corrective actions taken.

column4: Describe your organization's policies and commitments to halt and reverse biodiversity loss, including goals, targets, and how they align with global frameworks (e.g., Kunming-Montreal GBF).
column8: Describe all policies, commitments, and strategies in place to halt and reverse biodiversity loss. State alignment with global frameworks (e.g., Kunming-Montreal Global Biodiversity Framework, Taskforce on Nature-related Financial Disclosures).

column3: Operations in or near Sensitive Areas
column4: Does your organization have operations or sites located in or near biodiversity-sensitive areas (national parks, wildlife sanctuaries, wetlands, biosphere reserves, coastal regulation zones)? If yes, provide details.
column8: List operations or sites located in or adjacent to nationally or internationally protected areas or biodiversity-sensitive ecosystems. For each, describe the type of operations and compliance with environmental approvals or clearances.

column4: Has your organization conducted biodiversity impact assessments for its operations? Describe the assessment methodology, findings, and whether conducted by an independent agency.
column8: Describe biodiversity impact assessments conducted. Include methodology, scope, key findings (positive and negative impacts), whether assessments were by independent agencies, and whether results are publicly available.

column4: Describe the mitigation and restoration actions taken to address negative biodiversity impacts, applying the mitigation hierarchy (avoid, minimize, restore, offset).
column8: Describe actions taken to mitigate negative biodiversity impacts, applying the mitigation hierarchy: avoidance, minimization, restoration, and offsets/compensation. Report areas restored or under restoration.

column4: Identify your organization's dependencies on ecosystem services. Describe how these dependencies are managed and the risks they pose to your business.
column8: Identify ecosystem services on which operations depend (e.g., water provisioning, pollination, climate regulation). Describe how these dependencies are assessed, managed, and disclosed in risk frameworks.

column4: Report on species and habitats affected by your operations, including any species on the IUCN Red List impacted by your activities.
column8: Report species listed on the IUCN Red List or national equivalents that are impacted by the organization's activities. Describe habitats affected by operations and mitigation actions taken.

column4: Which forest-risk commodities (timber, palm oil, soy, cattle, cocoa, coffee, rubber) does your organization source? Describe the volumes, geographies, and sourcing channels.
column6: Metric tonnes (MT) or cubic metres; % by geography
column8: List all forest-risk commodities sourced (timber, palm oil, soy, cattle/leather, cocoa, coffee, rubber). For each, report volume sourced, geographies of origin, and sourcing channel (directly from producers vs. through traders).

column4: Describe your organization's approach to assessing deforestation and conversion risk in your supply chain, including tools, data sources, and coverage.
column8: Describe the approach, tools (e.g., Global Forest Watch, Trase, satellite monitoring), and data sources used to assess deforestation and ecosystem conversion risk in the supply chain. Report the proportion of supply chain covered.

column4: Has your organization made a commitment to eliminate deforestation from its supply chain? Describe the scope, timeline, and implementation strategy.
column8: State whether a no-deforestation or no-conversion commitment has been made. Describe the scope (commodities, geographies, supply chain tiers), target year, and implementation mechanism.

column4: What forest-related certifications does your organization hold or require from suppliers (e.g., FSC, PEFC, RSPO)? Describe the level of supply chain traceability achieved.
column8: Report the volume and percentage of forest-risk commodities sourced with recognized certifications (e.g., FSC, PEFC, RSPO, RTRS). Describe the level of supply chain traceability achieved (farm, mill, trader level).

column4: What are your organization's deforestation-free sourcing targets? Report progress against commitments and the percentage of supply chain covered.
column8: Report progress against deforestation-free sourcing targets. Include baseline data, current percentage of supply chain covered, and projected trajectory toward 100% compliance.

column4: Report the types and volumes of plastic your organization produces, uses, or commercializes in its operations and value chain.
column8: Report total volume of plastics used or produced across operations. Break down by polymer type where possible. Describe whether plastics are in products, packaging, or production processes.

column4: Report the volume of plastic packaging used and the proportion that is recyclable, recycled-content, or reusable. Describe packaging reduction targets.
column8: Report total plastic packaging used by type. State the percentage that is recyclable, recycled-content, or reusable. Describe reduction targets and timelines for transitioning to sustainable packaging alternatives.

column4: Report the volume of plastic waste generated, recycled, recovered, and disposed of during the reporting period.
column8: Report total plastic waste generated, recycled, recovered through other means, and sent to disposal (landfill or incineration). Report as absolute volumes and as percentages of total plastic waste generated.

column4: Describe initiatives to reduce plastic use, transition to alternative materials, or advance circular economy strategies for plastics in your operations.
column8: Describe specific initiatives to reduce plastic use, shift to alternative materials, or apply circular economy principles to plastics. For each initiative, report the outcome (volume of plastic reduced or avoided).

column4: Has your organization mapped where plastics are produced, used, and disposed of across your value chain? Describe the mapping scope and findings.
column8: State whether the organization has mapped where plastics are produced, commercialized, used, and disposed of across the value chain. Describe the scope of the mapping and key findings.

column4: Does your organization have a sustainable sourcing policy? What percentage of input materials are sourced sustainably? Describe the criteria and verification process used.
column8: Confirm whether a sustainable sourcing policy is in place. Report the percentage of inputs sourced sustainably. Describe the criteria used to define 'sustainable' and the verification or audit mechanism applied.

column4: What percentage of your suppliers have been assessed for environmental performance? Describe the assessment criteria, findings, and corrective actions taken.
column6: % of suppliers assessed; Number of corrective actions
column8: Report the percentage of suppliers (by value of business) assessed for environmental criteria. Describe the assessment methodology, key findings, and any corrective action plans implemented.

column4: What percentage of your suppliers have been assessed for social and human rights performance? Describe the assessment criteria, findings, and corrective actions taken.
column6: % of suppliers assessed; Number of corrective actions
column8: Report the percentage of suppliers (by value of business) assessed for social and human rights criteria (including labor rights, safety, child labor, forced labor). Describe findings and corrective actions taken.

column4: What percentage of your procurement (by value) is sourced from MSMEs, local producers, or suppliers within the district and neighbouring districts? Describe any preferential procurement policies.
column8: Report the percentage of total procurement (by value) sourced from MSMEs, small producers, and local suppliers (within the district or neighbouring districts). Describe any preferential procurement policy and its implementation.

column4: Do human rights requirements form part of your business agreements, contracts, and supplier onboarding processes? Describe how this is implemented and verified.
column8: State whether human rights clauses are embedded in standard supplier contracts and business agreements. Describe the specific requirements included and how compliance is verified or monitored.

column4: What training and awareness programs have been conducted for value chain partners on ESG principles and responsible business conduct? Report coverage by percentage of value of business done.
column8: Report the number and topics of ESG awareness programs conducted for value chain partners. Report coverage as a percentage of total value of business done with such partners. Describe training formats and outcomes.

column4: To what extent has your organization achieved supply chain transparency and traceability? Describe the scope, tools used, and commodities covered.
column8: Describe the level of supply chain transparency achieved. State the percentage of supply chain covered by traceability systems, the supply chain tiers mapped (tier 1, 2, 3+), and the tools or platforms used.

column4: Report the total number of employees and workers (permanent and non-permanent), broken down by gender, category, and whether differently abled.
column7: As at end of FY + Previous FY
column8: Report total headcount broken down by employment type (permanent/non-permanent), category (employees/workers), gender (male/female), and whether differently abled. Report as absolute numbers and percentages.

column4: Report the turnover rate for permanent employees and workers, broken down by gender, for the current and previous two financial years.
column8: Report turnover rates for permanent employees and workers, broken down by gender, for the current and two preceding financial years to show a 3-year trend.

column4: Report the number and percentage of employees and workers paid equal to or more than the minimum wage, by gender and employment category. Report median remuneration by gender and category.
column8: Report the number and percentage of employees and workers paid equal to or more than minimum wage, by gender and employment category. Report median remuneration/salary/wages for each category and gender.

column4: Report the percentage of employees and workers covered by health insurance, accident insurance, maternity benefits, paternity benefits, and daycare facilities. Describe retirement benefits (PF, gratuity, ESI) provided.
column8: Report the percentage of employees and workers covered by: health insurance, accident insurance, maternity benefits, paternity benefits, and daycare facilities. Also report coverage of retirement benefits (PF, Gratuity, ESI), broken down by permanent/non-permanent and gender.

column4: Report the return-to-work rate and retention rate of permanent employees and workers who took parental leave, broken down by gender.
column8: Report the return-to-work rate and the 12-month retention rate for permanent employees and workers who took parental leave, broken down by gender.

column4: Report the percentage of employees and workers who are members of recognized trade unions or associations. Describe the organization's approach to collective bargaining rights and freedom of association.
column8: Report the number and percentage of employees and workers who are members of recognized trade unions or collective bargaining associations. Describe the organization's approach to freedom of association and collective bargaining rights.

column4: Has your organization assessed its operations and supply chain for child labor risks? Report any incidents, corrective actions, and the percentage of plants/suppliers assessed.
column8: Report the number of child labor incidents identified in operations and supply chain. Report the percentage of plants/offices and suppliers assessed. Describe corrective actions taken.

column4: Has your organization assessed its operations and supply chain for forced or compulsory labor risks? Report any incidents, corrective actions, and the percentage of plants/suppliers assessed.
column8: Report the number of forced or compulsory labor incidents identified in operations and supply chain. Report the percentage of plants/offices and suppliers assessed. Describe corrective actions taken.

column4: Report the number and percentage of employees and workers who have received training on human rights issues and your organization's human rights policies.
column8: Report the number and percentage of employees and workers who received training on human rights issues and organizational human rights policies, broken down by employment category and gender.

column4: Describe the scope and process of your organization's human rights due diligence. Have any business processes been modified as a result of human rights grievances?
column8: Describe the scope and methodology of the organization's human rights due diligence process. State whether it covers the value chain and what remediation actions have been taken as a result.

column4: Report the number of complaints related to sexual harassment, discrimination, child labor, forced labor, and wage disputes filed during the reporting period and pending at year-end.
column8: Report complaints filed and pending resolution (at year-end) for: sexual harassment, discrimination at workplace, child labor, forced/involuntary labor, wage disputes, and other human rights issues.

column4: Report incidents of violations of indigenous peoples' rights related to your organization's operations. Describe corrective actions taken.
column8: Report any incidents of violations of indigenous peoples' rights related to organizational operations or land use. Describe the context, parties affected, and corrective actions taken or underway.

column4: Does your organization provide transition assistance programs (outplacement, retraining, retirement support) to employees facing career endings due to retirement or termination?
column8: State whether transition assistance programs are available for employees facing career endings (retirement, redundancy, or termination). Describe the type of support offered (outplacement, retraining, counselling, financial support).

column4: Has your organization implemented an occupational health and safety (OHS) management system? Describe its scope, coverage, and whether it is legally mandated or voluntary.
column8: Describe the OHS management system in place. State whether it is certified (e.g., ISO 45001), its scope of coverage (workers, contractors, visitors), and whether it is legally mandated or voluntary. Indicate if independently verified.

column4: Describe the processes used to identify work-related hazards and assess risks on both a routine and non-routine basis. How is the quality of these processes assured?
column8: Describe processes for identifying and assessing work-related hazards on a routine and non-routine basis. Include how the quality and effectiveness of these processes is assured and the tools or standards used.

column4: Describe the mechanisms that allow workers to report work-related hazards and remove themselves from unsafe situations without fear of reprisal.
column8: Describe the formal mechanisms through which workers can report hazards and remove themselves from unsafe work situations without facing disciplinary action or reprisal.

column4: Report the Lost Time Injury Frequency Rate (LTIFR) and total recordable work-related injuries for both employees and workers for the current and previous financial year.
column6: Rate per million hours worked; Number
column8: Report the Lost Time Injury Frequency Rate (LTIFR) and total recordable work-related injuries separately for employees and workers. Provide the number of hours worked as the denominator.

column4: Report the number of work-related fatalities among employees and workers during the reporting period.
column8: Report the total number of work-related fatalities separately for employees and workers. Describe the circumstances and the corrective or preventive actions implemented in response.

column4: Report cases of work-related ill-health and occupational diseases for employees and workers, including the types of diseases and rates.
column6: Number of cases; Rate per 1,000 workers
column8: Report work-related ill-health and occupational disease cases separately for employees and workers. Include disease types where known. Describe monitoring programs in place.

column4: Do employees and workers have access to non-occupational medical and healthcare services? Describe the scope and nature of healthcare access provided.
column8: State whether employees and workers have access to non-occupational medical and healthcare services. Describe the types of services provided (on-site clinic, health insurance, wellness programs) and the coverage.

column4: Report the number and percentage of employees and workers who received training on health and safety measures, by category and gender.
column8: Report the number and percentage of employees and workers who received OHS training during the reporting period, broken down by category (employees/workers) and gender.

column4: What percentage of your plants and offices have been assessed for health and safety practices and working conditions by internal teams, statutory authorities, or third parties?
column8: Report the percentage of plants and offices assessed for health and safety practices and working conditions during the reporting year, by assessment type (internal, statutory authority, third-party).

column4: Report the number of employees and workers who suffered high-consequence injuries and have been rehabilitated and placed in suitable employment, or whose family members have been placed in suitable employment.
column8: Report the total number of employees and workers who suffered high-consequence work-related injuries or ill-health, and the number who have been rehabilitated and placed in suitable employment or whose family members have been placed in employment.

column4: Report the number and percentage of women on the Board of Directors and in Key Management Personnel positions.
column8: Report the total number and percentage of women on the Board of Directors and in Key Management Personnel (KMP) positions. Trend data for 3 years is encouraged.

column4: Report the total number of employees and workers broken down by gender across all employment categories and levels.
column8: Report total employees and workers broken down by gender (male, female, and other/non-binary where applicable) across all employment categories. Report as both absolute numbers and percentages.

column4: Report the number and percentage of differently abled employees and workers (permanent and non-permanent), broken down by gender.
column8: Report the total number and percentage of differently abled employees and workers, broken down by permanent/non-permanent status and gender.

column4: Does your organization have an equal opportunity policy per the Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act (or equivalent)? Are your premises accessible to differently abled employees, workers, and visitors?
column8: State whether an equal opportunity policy is in place (per Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act 2016 or equivalent). Confirm whether physical premises are accessible to differently abled employees, workers, and visitors. Describe gaps and plans to address them.

column4: Report the number of discrimination incidents at the workplace during the current and previous financial year. Describe corrective actions taken.
column8: Report the number of discrimination incidents reported at the workplace during the current and previous financial year. Describe corrective actions taken and whether processes have been reviewed as a result.

column4: Report the number and percentage of employees and workers who received formal performance and career development reviews, broken down by gender.
column8: Report the number and percentage of employees and workers who received formal performance and career development reviews, broken down by employment category and gender.

column4: Report the percentage of board members, KMPs, employees, and workers covered by training on ESG principles, ethics, and anti-corruption during the financial year.
column8: Report the number of ESG, ethics, and anti-corruption training programs conducted during the year. Report the percentage of Board members, KMPs, employees, and workers covered, by category. Include topics covered and impact assessment.

column4: Report the number and percentage of employees and workers who received training on health and safety measures, by category.
column8: Report the number and percentage of employees and workers who received health and safety training, by category (employees/workers) and gender. Describe the content and format of programs offered.

column4: Report the total hours of skill development and professional training provided to employees and workers. Describe the types of programs offered.
column6: Total training hours; Average hours per employee
column8: Report total hours and average hours of skill development and professional training provided per employee, by category and gender. Describe the types of programs offered and investment made.

column4: Report the number of awareness programs conducted for value chain partners on ESG and responsible business conduct topics, and the percentage of partners covered.
column6: Number of programs; % of partners covered
column8: Report the number of ESG awareness and training programs conducted for value chain partners. Report coverage as a percentage of total value of business done with such partners. Describe the topics covered.

column4: Has your organization conducted Social Impact Assessments (SIA) for projects under applicable laws? Describe the methodology, whether conducted by an independent agency, and whether results are in the public domain.
column8: Report Social Impact Assessments (SIAs) conducted for projects under applicable laws. For each, provide project name, SIA notification number, date, whether conducted by an independent external agency, and whether results are publicly available.

column4: Provide details of any ongoing Rehabilitation and Resettlement (R&R) programs for project-affected families, including number of families, percentage covered, and amounts paid.
column8: Report all ongoing Rehabilitation and Resettlement (R&R) projects. For each, provide the project name, state, district, number of Project Affected Families (PAFs), percentage covered by R&R, and total amounts paid during the year.

column4: Describe mechanisms to receive and redress community grievances. Describe engagement with vulnerable and marginalized stakeholder groups and actions taken to address their concerns.
column8: Describe mechanisms available for communities to raise grievances and seek redress. Report engagement with vulnerable and marginalized groups and specific actions taken to address their concerns.

column4: Is CSR applicable to your organization under applicable laws? Report CSR expenditure, projects undertaken, and investments in aspirational or underserved districts.
column8: State whether CSR is applicable under Section 135 of the Companies Act (or equivalent). Report total CSR expenditure, projects undertaken, geographies covered, and investments in aspirational or underserved districts identified by government bodies.

column4: Report the total number of beneficiaries from CSR projects and the percentage from vulnerable or marginalized groups.
column8: For each CSR project, report the number of beneficiaries and the percentage who are from vulnerable or marginalized groups (women, scheduled castes/tribes, persons with disabilities, elderly, etc.).

column4: What percentage of your operations have implemented local community engagement programs, stakeholder engagement plans, or social impact assessments?
column8: Report the percentage of operations with active local community engagement programs. Describe the types of programs (community development, infrastructure, livelihood support) and outcomes achieved.

column4: Does your organization use or benefit from traditional knowledge in its products or processes? Describe benefit-sharing arrangements and any intellectual property disputes involving traditional knowledge.
column8: Identify any intellectual property derived from traditional knowledge that the organization owns or has acquired. For each, describe whether benefits are shared with originating communities and the basis for calculating the benefit share.

column4: What percentage of your significant product and service categories have been assessed for health and safety impacts? Report incidents of non-compliance with product safety regulations.
column6: % of product categories assessed; Number of incidents
column8: Report the percentage of significant product and service categories assessed for health and safety impacts. Report incidents of non-compliance with product safety regulations, including fines, warnings, and product bans.

column4: What percentage of your products and services carry information on environmental and social parameters, safe and responsible usage, and recycling or safe disposal guidance?
column8: Report the percentage of products and services (by number of SKUs or by turnover) that include labeling on: (a) environmental and social parameters, (b) safe and responsible usage, and (c) recycling or safe disposal guidance.

column4: Report incidents of non-compliance with regulations concerning product and service information, labeling, marketing communications, and advertising. Describe corrective actions taken.
column6: Number of non-compliances; INR / USD fines
column8: Report incidents of non-compliance with advertising and marketing regulations. For each, describe the nature of the non-compliance, regulatory action taken, and corrective measures implemented.

column4: Report the number of voluntary and forced product recalls during the reporting period and the reasons for each recall.
column8: Report the total number of voluntary and forced product recalls during the reporting period. For each recall, describe the product category, reason for recall, number of units affected, and actions taken to address the underlying safety issue.

column4: Describe the mechanisms in place to receive and respond to consumer complaints and feedback. Report the number of complaints filed and pending resolution by category.
column8: Describe the consumer complaint and feedback mechanism. Report complaints received and pending resolution at year-end by category: data privacy, advertising, cybersecurity, delivery of essential services, restrictive trade practices, unfair trade practices.

column4: Does your organization have a framework or policy on data privacy and cybersecurity? Report the number of data breaches, including those involving personally identifiable information (PII) of customers.
column8: State whether a data privacy and cybersecurity policy or framework exists and provide a web link. Report the number of substantiated data breaches, including those involving personally identifiable information (PII) of customers.

column4: Describe mechanisms in place to inform consumers of any risk of disruption or discontinuation of essential services.
column8: Describe the mechanisms in place to proactively inform customers of risks of disruption or discontinuation of essential services. Include communication channels used and response time commitments.

column4: Has your organization conducted consumer satisfaction surveys? Describe channels through which product and service information is made accessible to consumers.
column8: Describe whether and how consumer satisfaction is measured (surveys, NPS, complaints analysis). Report key findings and channels through which product and service information is made accessible to consumers.

column4: Report direct economic value generated and distributed (EVG&D), including revenues, operating costs, employee compensation, community investments, and retained earnings.
column8: Report Economic Value Generated and Distributed (EVG&D): revenues, operating costs, employee wages and benefits, payments to providers of capital, payments to governments (taxes), community investments, and retained earnings.

column4: Identify and report risks and opportunities posed by climate change that have the potential to generate substantive changes to your organization, including estimated financial implications.
column8: For each material climate-related financial risk or opportunity, describe its nature, time horizon, likelihood, and estimated financial impact (as a range). Describe management costs and strategic response.

column4: Report the percentage of R&D and capital expenditure (capex) invested in technologies to improve environmental and social impacts of products and processes.
column8: Report total R&D and Capex investments. Report separately the amount and percentage invested in technologies to improve environmental and social impacts. Describe the nature of these investments.

column4: Has your organization conducted a Life Cycle Assessment (LCA) for any products or services? Report the boundary, whether conducted by an independent agency, and whether results are publicly available.
column8: Report Life Cycle Assessments (LCAs) conducted for products or services. For each, provide the product/service name, NIC code, percentage of turnover, boundary of the LCA, whether conducted by an independent agency, and public availability of results.

column4: Is your organization compliant with applicable environmental laws and regulations (Water Act, Air Act, Environment Protection Act, and equivalent)? Report any non-compliances, regulatory actions, and corrective measures.
column8: State whether the organization is compliant with all applicable environmental laws (Water Act, Air Act, Environment Protection Act, and associated rules). Report any non-compliances, fines, regulatory orders, and corrective actions taken.

column4: Has your organization conducted Environmental Impact Assessments (EIA) for projects under applicable regulations? Provide details including notification number, date, whether by independent agency, and public disclosure status.
column8: Report all Environmental Impact Assessments conducted for projects under applicable notification requirements. For each, provide the project name, EIA notification number, date, whether conducted by an independent external agency, and public disclosure status.

column4: Is your organization compliant with applicable social and labor laws (rights of differently abled persons, human rights legislation, labor regulations)? Report any non-compliances and corrective actions.
column8: State whether the organization is compliant with applicable social and labor laws (Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act, human rights legislation, minimum wages, labor regulations). Report non-compliances and corrective actions.

column4: Report all instances of non-compliance with environmental, social, or governance regulations during the reporting period, including penalties imposed and corrective actions taken or underway.
column6: Number of instances; INR / USD penalties
column8: Report all instances of regulatory non-compliance across environmental, social, and governance domains. For each, describe the applicable law, nature of the non-compliance, penalty or action taken, and corrective measures underway or completed.
